differentials.name: Cervical thymic cyst = persistent thymopharyngeal duct
differentials.raw: Cervical thymic cyst = persistent thymopharyngeal duct

context: It is critical to identify common congenital lesions like branchial cleft + thyroglossal duct cysts, and to recognize their complications (eg, superinfection) √  NO thyroid tissue in normal location √  thin fibrous band attaching it to an orthotopic gland √  transspatial extent containing locules Age:   congenital lesion detected by age 2 years Location:   near mandibular angle along anterior surface of sternocleidomastoid muscle, lateral to carotid sheath structures, posterior to submandibular gland √  round mass displacing sternocleidomastoid muscle posterolaterally √  PATHOGNOMONIC beak ins
